headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 and the Mythos family dominates this cycle, with educators racing to demo and frame the new models.
body: Cyril Imhof leads the recommended coverage, presenting Fable 5 as distinct from Opus and centering the "know your agent" concept alongside agent design patterns and context engineering for long running applications [0][1]. Chase AI and Claude's own channel fill in specifics: Mythos 5 and Fable 5 are now generally available, with Fable 5 described as free on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before shifting to usage credits, and Mythos framed as Fable with guardrails [7][10]. Claude's demos push the model into autonomous agent territory, playing Factorio without human intervention, simulating the solar system from physics first principles to predict eclipses, and generating code for a fluid simulation set to a Beethoven remix [11][12][13]. Among the other recommended Better Stack videos, the standouts are Headroom, a Netflix engineer's open-source compressor that cuts LLM token usage by 60-95 percent, and Open Notebook, a self-hosted NotebookLM alternative with an API [5][2].
